examples.vote_description: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2022
examples.reason: Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act of 2022 — reauthorizes job training programs that unions support as they fund apprenticeships and workforce development.

examples.vote_description: Improving Interagency Coordination for Pipeline Reviews Act
examples.reason: Improving Interagency Coordination for Pipeline Reviews Act — streamlines federal review process for pipeline projects, directly benefiting oil and gas pipeline companies.

examples.vote_description: Streamlining Procurement for Effective Execution and Delivery and National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2026
examples.reason: FY2026 NDAA (National Defense Authorization Act) — authorizes military spending, procurement, and personnel. Defense contractors strongly support annual NDAA passage as it funds weapons systems and military programs.

examples.vote_description: Protecting Prudent Investment of Retirement Savings Act
examples.reason: Protecting Prudent Investment of Retirement Savings Act — despite the name, this ROLLS BACK the DOL ESG investing rule, restricting how pension funds (including union pension funds) can consider environmental and social factors when investing. Unions strongly opposed this bill.

examples.vote_description: To repeal section 134 of the Clean Air Act, relating to the greenhouse gas reduction fund
examples.reason: Repeals EPA Greenhouse Gas Reduction Fund (the green bank created by IRA) — eliminates $27B fund for clean energy projects, directly benefiting fossil fuels by removing clean energy subsidies.

highlights.issue: Universal background checks for gun purchases
Build a wall on the U.S.-Mexico border
Require permits to carry concealed guns
